core_mechanism: a goodness-of-fit / discrepancy test comparing an empirical frequency distribution against a fixed scale-invariant reference distribution (the logarithmic first-digit law), with threshold-based flagging of significant deviations as anomalies, applied separately to directional sub-components
brief_reason: The abstract detects tampering by measuring how the observed density's leading-digit frequencies deviate from Benford's Law using adapted threshold values, splitting the data into directional components to localize where the empirical distribution departs from the expected reference.
